title: Technical Delivery Plan
description: Structured implementation plan produced by Technical Lead. Consumed by Backend Developer, Frontend Developer, Mobile Engineer, QA Engineer, Reviewer, Agent Coordinator, and Technical Writer. One plan per feature or major slice grouping. Slices map directly to implementation-result.json emits.

plan_id.description: Unique plan identifier. Convention: YYYY-MM-DD-slug or project-ref.

feature_ticket_ref.description: ticket_id from feature-ticket.json this plan implements.

adr_refs.description: ADR IDs or paths that constrain this implementation.

goal_summary.description: What must be true when this plan is done. Expressed as outcomes, not activities.

preserved_behavior.description: Behavior that must remain unchanged after all slices complete. Directly from feature-ticket.json.

slices.description: Ordered delivery slices. Each slice produces one implementation-result.json.

slice_id.description: Unique slice identifier within this plan.

description.description: What this slice does and what files/behavior it changes.

owner_role.description: Role responsible for this slice (e.g. backend-developer, frontend-developer).

depends_on.description: slice_ids that must complete before this slice starts.

output_schema_ref.description: Schema the slice owner must emit on completion (usually implementation-result).

risk_level.description: Risk level of this slice for reviewer prioritization.

impact_radius.description: Services, APIs, event consumers, clients, or teams affected by this change.

quality_gates.description: Minimum quality gate requirements before delivery is considered done.

tests.description: Test coverage requirement (e.g. 'unit tests for all new business logic', 'integration test for COD routing').

review.description: Review requirement (e.g. 'at least 1 Reviewer approval', 'Security Engineer review for auth changes').

security.description: Security check requirement, or 'none' if not applicable.

observability.description: Instrumentation requirement (e.g. 'OTel spans on new integration points') or 'not_required'.

rollout_notes.description: Deployment sequencing, feature flag requirements, canary strategy, or mixed-version considerations.

rollback_notes.description: How to revert if something goes wrong post-deploy. Include whether rollback is safe or destructive.

documentation_deltas.description: Topics or pages requiring documentation updates — handed off to Technical Writer.

open_questions.description: Unresolved questions that could affect delivery — escalate to Technical Architect or BA if blocking.

readiness_status.description: Current plan status for Agent Coordinator phase gate tracking.

examples.work_title: COD Store Order Assignment Logic — DMS Orders Service
examples.goal_summary: COD orders with store_id route to store courier; non-COD orders unchanged; fallback to warehouse when no store courier available.
examples.preserved_behavior: Non-COD order auto-assign flow is unchanged end-to-end.

slices.description: Modify NewLogicAutoAssign.php to add COD store routing branch. Add store courier lookup. Implement fallback with warning log.
Write unit tests for COD routing branch: happy path, fallback, non-COD isolation.

quality_gates.tests: Unit tests for all 3 AC scenarios from feature ticket; PHPUnit pass
quality_gates.review: 1 Reviewer approval; check COD branch isolation from existing code path
quality_gates.observability: not_required for this slice; add if store courier API call is external

examples.rollout_notes: No feature flag required. Change is additive. Deploy to staging first; run smoke test with COD + non-COD order samples.
examples.rollback_notes: Revert commit is safe — no migration, no schema change, no persistent side effects.
documentation_deltas: Update DMS order assignment runbook with COD routing rules
open_questions: Confirm whether store manager notification on fallback is in scope for v1 or deferred.
